hypothesis: eBPF Bytecode Modification:** Replace standard conditional jump instructions (`BPF_JMP | BPF_JGT` or `BPF_JLT`) in the partition kernel with predicated ALU64 arithmetic. Specifically, inject bytecode that computes a bitwise mask from the sign bit of a subtraction (`BPF_ALU64 | BPF_SUB`), shifts it (`BPF_ALU64 | BPF_RSH`), and uses it to conditionally swap elements and increment pointers using purely arithmetic instructions (`BPF_ALU64 | BPF_ADD`, `BPF_AND`). This forces the CPU to evaluate both sides of the partition condition simultaneously without branching.
